Hi! I'm Maryam Khalid.

Applied Scientist at Amazon AGI.
Specialized in |
I build intelligent systems across sensing, health, and AI, from raw signals to reliable decisions. Recently, I've been focused on AI trust and reliability, building LLM-as-a-Judge systems, RLHF quality loops, and agentic fact-checking pipelines for Amazon's Nova foundation models.
⌄

About Me

I'm an Applied Scientist at Amazon AGI with a PhD in Electrical & Computer Engineering from Rice University. My research bridges AI evaluation, human-centered sensing, and computational modeling.

Prior to Amazon, I spent some time at Google in the consumer health (wearable sleep recommendations) team. I was also selected as D2K Research Fellow where I led 4 client-facing ML deployments for industry clients.

Human-Centered AI & Sensing
Human-Centered AI & Sensing
Understanding humans through their data — behavior modeling, social networks, health, and personalization
Circadian Rhythm, Health Behaviors & Wellbeing
Google · Consumer Health · 2023
Chronobiology Intl 2025Deployed
Expand
Designed multimodal ML pipeline fusing physiological signals (heart rate, accelerometer, sleep) from 17K+ users to extract circadian phenotypes at scale. Engineered 5 reusable feature transformation tools normalizing cross-device heterogeneity into production-grade embeddings. Deployed end-to-end system powering personalized health recommendations for millions of users. Published Chronobiology International.
17K+
Participants
5
Tools built
45+
Features engineered
Paper (Chronobiology Intl)
SleepNet: Sleep Prediction via Dynamic Social Networks
Rice University · PhD Research
UbiComp 2024
Expand
Invented graph attention architecture (GEDD) for temporal social network modeling, combining dynamic subgraph extraction with sequential state prediction. Achieved 15% over SOTA across 110+ NIH-funded participants with 50% robustness to missing data—key for real-world deployment under sparse observations. Published UbiComp 2024; Harvard collaboration.
15%
Over SOTA
110+
Participants
50%
Missing data robust
ACMarXiv
Social Graph Networks for Emotion Prediction
Rice University
Nature SR 2023
Expand
Designed graph neural network architecture aggregating temporal social dynamics and multi-scale interaction patterns for real-time emotion prediction from mobile sensing networks. Demonstrates scalable graph-based inference on dynamic, evolving network structures. Published Nature Scientific Reports 2023.
Nature Scientific Reports
GRAIL: Graph Active Learning in Dynamic Sensing Environments
Rice University · PhD Research · 2025
arXiv 2025
Expand
Benchmarked 11 active learning strategies across 3 GNN architectures (GAT, GCN, GraphSAGE) for sample-efficient learning in dynamic environments. Reduced annotation burden 40%—directly applicable to reward labeling efficiency in RLHF and active exploration in RL. Novel evaluation framework quantifying exploration-exploitation trade-offs for production deployment. Published arXiv 2025.
11
SOTA strategies benchmarked
40%
Reduced annotation burden
3
GNN architectures
PaperarXiv
Predicting Stress in Caregivers
Rice University
ACII 2023
Expand
Developed longitudinal ML model with counterfactual causal reasoning for stress prediction and intervention optimization. Generates interpretable, actionable guidance for adaptive health interventions—analogous to policy optimization in sequential decision-making. Published ACII 2023.
Alzheimer's Biomarker Discovery
D2K Fellow · Rice · Hospital partner
Deployed
Expand
Delivered end-to-end ML diagnostic pipeline (>90% accuracy) from clinical and plasma biomarkers to hospital deployment. Led cross-functional team of 6 through full production delivery cycle—requirements, modeling, validation, and clinical integration.
Real-Time Cardiac Output from ICU Waveforms
D2K Fellow · Rice · Hospital partner
Deployed
Expand
Deployed real-time cardiac output inference from high-frequency ICU waveforms (<50ms latency, <5% RMSE). Engineered low-latency streaming signal processing pipeline optimized for real-time clinical decision support. Led team of 6 through end-to-end delivery.
<5%

DREAMR: Genomic Analysis Library
D2K Fellow · Rice University
Deployed
Expand
Engineered DREAMR, a production-grade genomic analysis library for macular degeneration diagnosis. Translated research algorithms into reusable, modular clinical software—bridging the research-to-production gap for genomic ML.
Computational Sensing & Modeling
Computational Sensing & Modeling
The mathematical foundations — signal detection, probabilistic models, and the theory that underpins everything
Probabilistic Modeling for Molecular Communication & Viral Aerosol Detection
ITU · KAUST · Rice · 2017–2020
IEEE Trans. Comm.IEEE Comm. Mag.Best Readings in MC
Expand
Developed stochastic optimization and probabilistic mathematical models (Navier-Stokes PDEs, advection-diffusion) for aerosol viral transmission with ML-based detection theory. Engineered biosensor receiver architecture; formulated maximum-likelihood estimation under uncertainty. Combines operations research, statistical modeling, and signal processing. 3 IEEE publications (ICC, Trans. Comm., Comm. Mag.). Featured in "Best Readings in Molecular Communication." Gold Medal M.S. thesis.
3
IEEE publications
PDE
Channel modeling
ML
Detection theory
arXiv (Comm. Mag.)IEEE Trans. Comm.
RL-Based Trajectory Optimization for Autonomous Drone Sensing
Rice University · 2019
HPC Oil & Gas 2019Demo'd
Expand
Designed deep RL flight planning system (Q-network with experience replay) for autonomous drone networks, optimizing multi-agent exploration-exploitation under uncertainty for real-time hazard prediction. Developed physics-informed system identification from sparse sensor observations. Combines sequential decision-making, multi-agent coordination, and real-time optimization. Demonstrated at HPC Oil & Gas 2019. Led team of 3.
RL
